description: Animal skin preserved and altered for use as a material is 'kori'. Tanning and other treatments change its resistance, flexibility, surface, and smell. 'hisae' (skin) names the body covering; leather names what that covering becomes through material work.
articulatory_notes: The back of the tongue closes against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and releases into stressed rounded 'o'. A tap or brief trill then turns the voice into high-front 'i'.
usage_notes: The animal and tanning method identify leather's source. Finish and care describe the object in hand.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A leather shoe began as an animal's skin. Keeping that source in the definition lets a material account discuss use, care, and harm without allowing the finished surface to hide its origin.

semantic_domains.creation: preserved animal skin worked into durable flexible objects
semantic_domains.physical: animal skin altered by tanning or another preservation process
